zh.Cromarty, Ross & Cromarty, Comartyshire,: 愛爾蘭庫迪 科克羅斯
zh.Berwick-Upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Engl: 英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德貝維其可-朋-特維德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lillias De Ross於1329年11月29日出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克羅斯時，她的父親Earl Hugh Ross of Ross54歲，母親Margaret de Graham34歲。她於1348年與William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1366年在蘇格蘭享年37歲。

zh-hans.Cromarty, Ross & Cromarty, Comartyshire,: 爱尔兰庫迪 科克羅斯
zh-hans.Berwick-Upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Engl: 英国英格兰諾圖波藍德貝維其可-朋-特維德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lillias De Ross于1329年11月29日出生在爱尔兰庫迪 科克羅斯时，她的父亲Earl Hugh Ross of Ross54岁，母亲Margaret de Graham34岁。她于1348年与William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty在英国苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1366年在苏格兰享年37岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ross
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Ruskin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Tauba, Uzi.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rosce (see Ross 4) + the diminutive suffix -kin.
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname, for example   Raskin  .

variants.name: Ros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rose
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rossell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rusling
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rocelin (Old French Roscelin, Rocelin, ancient Germanic Ruozelin), a double diminutive of Rozo (see Ross ).

variants.name: Rohs
meanings: German: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Joris.
German: topographic name from Middle High German rœsse ‘kiln’, in particular one used for drying hemp and flax or for burning lime.
English and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Ross  .

nameMeanings.name: Lillias
meanings: An English female name. Same as Lilias. From Latin, lilium, a lily.

en.lifeSummary: When Lillias De Ross was born on 29 November 1329, in Ross, County Cork, Ireland, her father, Earl Hugh Ross of Ross, was 54 and her mother, Margaret de Graham, was 34. She married William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty in 1348, in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. She died in 1366, in Scotland, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lillias De Ross est née le 29 novembre 1329, à Ross, County Cork, Irlande, son père, Earl Hugh Ross of Ross, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Graham, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty en 1348, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1366, à Écosse, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lillias De Ross am 29. November 1329 in Ross, County Cork, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Earl Hugh Ross of Ross 54 und ihre Mutter Margaret de Graham 34. Sie hat 1348 in Schottland, United Kingdom William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1366 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lillias De Ross è nata il il 29 novembre 1329 nel Ross, County Cork, Irlanda, sua padre, Earl Hugh Ross of Ross, aveva 54 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Graham, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty nel 1348, nel Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1366, nel Scotland, all'età di 37 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lillias De Rossが1329年11月29日にアイルランドコウントイ コルクロスで生まれた時，父Earl Hugh Ross of Rossは54歳，母Margaret de Grahamは34歳であった。1348年にイギリススコットランドでWilliam Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromartyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1366年にスコットランドで37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lillias De Ross(이)가 1329년 11월 29일 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 로스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Earl Hugh Ross of Ross(은)는 54세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Graham(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1348년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1366년 스컷랜드에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Berwick-Upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Engl: Berwick-upon-Tweed, Norðhymbra land, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lillias De Ross nasceu em 29 de novembro de 1329, em Ross, County Cork, Irlanda, seu pai, Earl Hugh Ross of Ross, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Graham, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty em 1348, em Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1366, em Scotland, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lillias De Ross родилась 29 ноября 1329 г. в местности росс, коунти корк, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Earl Hugh Ross of Ross было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Graham, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty в 1348 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1366 г. в местности Шотландия в возрасте 37 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lillias De Ross nació el 29 de noviembre de 1329, en Ross, County Cork, Irlanda, su papá, Earl Hugh Ross of Ross, tenía 54 años y su mamá, Margaret de Graham, tenía 34 años. Se casó con William Urquhart de Urchard 1st of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty en 1348, en Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió en 1366, en Scotland, a la edad de 37 años.
